author: R B
msg: So today the rewards for 6 x s8 farm devices on Ethernet and 1x personal iPhone connected to the same ISP are around 10cents to 18 cents massively lower but not awful

author: Forty41
msg: I tethered a couple of phones to other phones with sims today . The phones which were tethering saw a drop in rewards but overall they averaged 21 cents . Highest was an eSIM phone I was using for S&R at 48c and lowest 11 cents. All other phones still dust with many phones connected to broadband and sim routers .

author: Abdus
msg: I think they just should make it a legal disclosure. You want to use the app, then data about the phone has to be collected. Your choice. Don’t consent, app locks and you can’t use it. Request to clear stored identifiers, sure clear and lock up. You want in again? Ok consent to info about your phone used. That way you can tie down the “malicious” activity to the specific phone. Someone in verified posted their ULO is cloning the app and still receiving rewards. GDPR is a pain true but we can work with it.

author: Abdus
msg: Issue is the costs involved can be high and prohibitive. But that’s the way it is.

author: Abdus
msg: I hope this “quality” issue is just telemetry specific. They have claimed other tasks would benefit from “screenless phones” setup.

author: Kevin Blowers
msg: Once you removed the battery, how were you powering them?? I have successfully done this to moto g7 and g8. I might be able to help.

author: Flippy
msg: Fucking shitshow

author: GliTch_
msg: direct wire from the motherboard basically one of these boxes

author: GliTch_
msg: where they have the low voltage out on a lead that plugs into the boards

author: GliTch_
msg: I would get them to "charge" but whenever I powered them on they would just shutdown

author: Kevin Blowers
msg: So did you have 2 sets of wires going to the phone. 1 plugged into the usb c connection for data transfer and a red and black onto the phone where the original battery was connected to?

author: GliTch_
msg: yeah

author: Kevin Blowers
msg: What I done will all of mine was to carefully remove the pcb off each battery. Then solder the wires that came with the box onto the + and - connections on the pcb. This means I was able to use the original connector from the battery to ensure all connections were the same. If you do this be careful as the batteries can be volatile

author: GliTch_
msg: yeah that was going to be my next step

author: Kevin Blowers
msg: I carefully cut the connection between the battery and the pcb with precision wire cutters.
